orgDesign.companyProfile: Typical mid-size to large enterprise with dedicated revenue, marketing, support, finance, workplace, security, engineering, and enterprise-data organizations.
orgDesign.operatingModel: The internal domain is organized around six value areas: revenue and growth, service/operations/finance, control/risk/trust, product/technology/enablement, executive and capital management, and employee/workplace productivity. Stream-aligned teams own specific journeys inside those areas, while shared platform and data teams provide reusable foundations.
designPrinciples: Each team group should map clearly to one of the domain root areas used in the product-brick taxonomy.
Commercial, service, finance, workplace, and governance workflows stay close to the teams accountable for their operating KPIs.
Shared platform and data capabilities remain reusable foundations rather than local point solutions.
orgDesign.staffingPrinciple: Prefer teams of roughly 8-11 FTE, with broader customer streams split into multiple sibling teams inside the same group or team family.
orgDesign.groupingPrinciple: Name team groups so they clearly roll up into the six canonical internal-domain root areas, while preserving concrete team-family ownership.
orgDesign.groupLeadershipPrinciple: When a group contains several 10-FTE teams, add lightweight group-level leadership and principal IC roles rather than scaling any single team too far beyond its local scope.

groups.name: Revenue & Growth / Revenue Operations Platform

roles.responsibility: Own the roadmap, prioritization, and KPI tradeoffs for this team scope.
